markdownRemark.html:
I am currently building the website for my wedding next year which has to be translated into English, German and French to make the information accessible for all our guests.

From work I know about the significance of internationalization (i18n) but also about the complexity of the topic. Which is why I started to look for libraries that were lightweight and easy to use. I quickly found myself in a deep rabbit hole, comparing the feature set of the big libraries like react-intl and i18next and small ones like polyglot.js. react-intl would be my go-to solution for a production app but I found that its setup and the translation format would be overkill for my tiny website. The same was true for i18next and polyglot.js also had too much functionality for my needs (e.g. support for all kinds of pluralizations that I did not require).

  • building a side project, small react app, overwhelmed by libraries and formats
  • aware of all formats, too tedious for something that will have only 30 strings

  • how to detect locale

    • best would be accept-language header
    • navigator
    • simple parsing of, yolo
    • not nuanced on purpose, should only support small numbers of languanges

  • explain t() and strings {}

    • regular strings
    • interpolation
    • plurals
  • usage in React
  • don’t use it in prod, please -> source of XSS
  • glith project
